messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How do you disable the USB port located on the back side of the machine?
Select <Off>.

chunk_text:
![4 Select <Off>](images/image_p97_00.png)

**<Off>**

Disables the USB port located on the back side of the machine.

Enables the USB port located on the back side of the machine.

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How can you specify the period of time after which auto sleep is performed?
You can set the duration between 3 to 10 or up to 60 minutes.

chunk_text:
If the machine has not been operated or has had no data to process for a specified period of time, the machine enters
sleep mode to save energy consumption (Auto Sleep). Specify the period of time after which auto sleep is performed.
Auto Sleep is not performed when the setting menu is displayed. We recommend using the factory default settings to
Entering Sleep Mode(P. 39) save the most power.

3 to 10 to 60 (min.)

messages.content: You are a Canon technical support specialist.
How do you set the auto shutdown time to automatically shut down the machine after idle?
Navigate to <Menu>, then select <Timer Settings> and choose <Auto Shutdown Time>. You can enter a value from 0 (disabled) or 1 to 8 hours, then press <Apply>.

chunk_text:
**down the machine after the specified period of idle time has elapsed since the Set a timer to automatically shut
machine entered sleep mode. Setting Auto Shutdown Time(P. 38)**

0 (Auto Shutdown is disabled.)
1 to 4 to 8 (hr.)


![Auto Shutdown Time](images/image_p107_00.png)

<Menu> <Timer Settings> <Auto Shutdown Time> Set the Auto Shutdown time

<Apply>

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
How do you adjust the printer density to appropriate levels for copying and printing reports?
Navigate through the menu by selecting Adjustment/Maintenance, then Printer Density, make your adjustment, and select Apply.

messages.content: You are a helpful Canon printer technician.
How can you skip the steps of opening a separate application for receiving scanned images?
By using applications that work directly with the machine, as shown in the image.

chunk_text: you're working on. This way you can skip the steps of opening a separate application just for receiving the. scanned images and then exporting them to the application you're really using.. ![Some applications work with the machine](images/image_p192_02.png). **For more information about this function, see Scanning Using an Application(P. 84) .**
